name.es: Pain rond (panecillo para el pan bagnat)

description.en: The firm-crusted, dense-crumbed round roll of Nice, built to soak up olive oil and hold a Niçoise salad filling without falling apart. This is the plain bread itself, the base of the famous pan bagnat sandwich.
description.de: Das feste, dichtkrumige Rundbrötchen aus Nizza, gemacht, um Olivenöl aufzusaugen und eine Salade-niçoise-Füllung zu tragen, ohne auseinanderzufallen. Dies ist das reine Brot selbst, die Grundlage des berühmten Pan-Bagnat-Sandwiches.
description.es: El panecillo redondo de corteza firme y miga densa de Niza, hecho para absorber aceite de oliva y sostener un relleno de ensalada niçoise sin deshacerse. Este es el pan solo, la base del famoso sándwich pan bagnat.
description.fr: Le petit pain rond à la croûte ferme et à la mie dense de Nice, fait pour absorber l'huile d'olive et tenir une garniture de salade niçoise sans se défaire. Voici le pain seul, la base du célèbre sandwich pan bagnat.

origin.note: 'Pan bagnat' means 'wet bread' in old Niçois, the name of the finished sandwich, born as portable food for 19th-century fishermen and market workers. This entry is the plain roll itself.

keeping.note: Needs to hold its structure for a day, pan bagnat is famously better the next day once the filling's oil has soaked in.

method.title: Bloom
method.summary: Dissolve the yeast in warm water and let it bubble a few minutes.

method.title: Mix
method.summary: Work in the flour, salt and olive oil; knead about ten minutes until smooth.

method.title: Shape
method.summary: Divide in two, shape into tight balls, then flatten slightly into rounds about 20cm across.

method.title: Bake
method.summary: Bake with steam until golden and firm, sturdy enough to hold a well-soaked Niçoise filling.

source.adaptation: Salt increased slightly from the source's volumetric 3g to 5g (2%) for standard French bread-salting practice.
